description: In Javanese, the voiceless velar stop /k/ has a glottal stop allophone [ʔ] in certain contexts, particularly word-finally and before another consonant. This represents a common allophonic process where the place of articulation shifts to the glottis in weak positions.
sources: Uhlenbeck, E. M. (1964). _A Critical Survey of Studies on the Languages of Java and Madura_. The Hague: Martinus Nijhoff.
Klamer, M. (2001). _Ejectives in Eastern Indonesian languages_. In _Proceedings of the 4th International Symposium on Indonesian Languages and Linguistics_.
Yannuar, N., Klamer, M., & Smith, K. (2022). _Examining Javanese Phonology through Word-Reversal Practices_. Oceanic Linguistics, 61(1), 1-24.
